text: For an intro course, this class was very difficult. The difficulty ramps up very quickly. Most of the prerecorded lectures are reused from previous years. Tests are fairly challenging requiring a solid grasp on the material. Dr. Ali is helpful during office hours and tries her best to help her students. Avoid taking online if possible!
Dr. Ali is great! She is caring towards her students and is willing to help if the class is not doing very well. She is also super helpful during office hours. I would take her again.

I would never take her for this class or any class again. She does not give us enough time for exams, one hour for 25 questions and having to write code for over 3 questions was ridiculous. She gives zeros to anyone she THINKS used AI. Lectures felt unorganized, and her exam reviews did not reflect what would be on the actual test
Never Take Her....

Very caring and engaging professor. Her working on problems with the class and drawing the concepts out makes the content much more engaging and fun. The tests are not bad if you understand the content.

Good and smart professor, she explained some topics very well but others... not really. She struggled a lot with the length of the class so some topics were not explained with enough details, which made the class harder. Homework and exams were hard but fair (easier than CS1). On another note, mandatory attendance for labs was awful (10% of grade).

I DO NOT recommend her. Her final felt unfair as it's only 60 mins for 47 questions including 5 long written response code. I studied so much for this course, giving it many hours. If you want a chill and easier side of a professor don't take her! Also she'll catch you with AI,I didn't even use it for advice or examples, couldn't risk getting a 0..

She is very kind and clearly cares about her students. She moves quickly through topics and expects a lot of outside studying. The workload is heavy with frequent quizzes, exams, and homework. However, she curves generously since averages are usually very low. Super hard class, but I'd recommend her and would take her again.

As a non cs student, I was worried about this class but Dr Ali made it easy to pass. Just watch the lectures and use them to write your codes. Start the assignments when they're posted so you have time to ask questions if needed. Exams were hard but doable.

She has the most important aptitude that a professor should have... communication. Great communication. The best I've ever had. But everyone knows communication sometimes doesnt cut it, which is why this other quality makes it perfect. She is extremely caring. She wants you to succeed, and she doesn't leave anyone behind. If you can take her, do.

- Be aware of your own risk this professor doesn't care of your grade and don't teach you half of the material and her lectures is really pointless if you wanna pass she told numerous of students that they complain and she has an bad attitude as well .

Said the final would be similar to the earlier exams, then gave us a 47-question test in 60 minutes, which is more questions and less time than the first two. Assigned homework over Thanksgiving break and didn't explain what to expect on the final until 2 days before finals. I'd call it poorly organized, but there wasn't any to begin with.

Awesome professor. Super nice and really helpful during office hours. Definitely cares about students. Live coding and explains well (sometimes gets a little lost but if you pay attention you'll be fine). CS1 is a hard class but she simplified the topics and gave tons of practice. 10/10.

I have never done a Rate My Professor before, but she is a genuinely awful professor. Did you add a comment in your code? Oh that's AI and no way you did that. Talk to your peer for help on assignment questions? Get 50% Here is your exam review. Take the exam and notice how it is nothing like it. Bro Code teaches better in 5-10 minutes.
Just do not take CS2 with her!!!!

- Genuinely good hearted professor
- Trying to use AI Detection system that is flagging innocent students
- Students are now putting more effort into preparing for validation checks proving they didn't cheat than actually doing assignments and learning.
If you try in her class you will get a zero for "using AI."
Great professor, was her first time and she did great. Her explanations were sometimes not the best but she really tries to help her students. Very sweet and kind person.

Dr. Ali is honestly one of the best CS professors I've had so far. She really cares about her students and them succeeding in her class. Her exams are fair just go over the practice problems she gives out and you will be just fine. She also explains things very well at her office hours and is very generous with partial credit on her exams.
Teacher was good at her job. I personally didn't go to class but the class was fairly okay to get by as long as you reviewed enough. Much less stressful than the other CS1 teachers.

professor simply can't code and honestly seems lost. she's nice, but knows so little that every time i asked something she just went, “i'm not sure.” her exam review was from another prof and had nothing to do with her actual tests, so i had no clue what'd be on it. got a B+ but wouldn't take her again. Instead, take Ahmed, Steinberg, or Gerber.

Ali is an amazing professor and cares about her students. Great lectures with plenty of examples conceptual AND coding. There are programming assignments that align great with what we are learning as well as quizzes that are multiple tries. She does a lot of practice from the FE and emphasizes preparing us for it. I learned so much in this class!

Dr Ali is very kind and caring Professor who wants you to succeed. Her tests are super chill and fair which is a breath of fresh air for the students that aren't in CS but still prepares CS students well for the FE.

Review is for COT4210. I have never written a review on here for any professor but I had to go out of my way this time for Dr. Ali. 
Although it was her first time ever teaching this course, she made a huge effort in making sure she was conveying the correct material and making sure we understood everything. Her lectures were also recorded! Amazing

I was worried about failing this class as I'm mediocre at coding, but Dr. Ali is truly the best. She really cares and wants you to pass, and is genuinely one of the kindest, coolest professors I've ever had at UCF. I'd highly recommend her for this class and any other class she teaches. She is the epitome of what a CS professor should strive to be!

Dr. Ali was so thoughtful and caring this whole semester. She responds so quickly to emails and is genuinely kind. her lectures are pretty good and she explains the concepts in CS1 well. Her lectures were recorded as well. I would definitely have her as a professor again if given the chance

Ali is a very good professor; CS1 is a difficult class regardless but Ali is one of the best options you can take to pass the class. I was expecting to leave this class with a C at the start of the semester but I ended up getting an A.

Dr. Ali is a wonderful professor, and did an amazing job for her first semester teaching. She gave good lectures and resources, listened to and implemented feedback from her students (like recording lectures), and overall is a kind and caring person. Exams can be tough, but she was generous with curves. Overall, she's an excellent professor.

This is my 3rd time taking CS1 and let me say Dr. Ali has been the most wonderful professor I have had. She truly takes her time to make sure you understand and truly wants you to pass. Her lectures are easy to understand and her exams are very similar to the study guide. I feel so prepared for the FE all thanks to Dr. Ali.

Given that this was her first semester as a professor, she was wonderful. Extremely caring and respectful, she put a lot of effort into the class and really wanted us to learn and succeed. Some of the concepts were difficult to understand and she wasn't always amazing at explaining them, but I imagine she will get better with experience.

Dr Ali was a really good teacher for it being her first semester. She really cares about the success of her students. The assignments we're fair with enough time to complete them and focused mainly on the topics she was teaching at the time. Her exams we're fair and focused more on concepts. She was also really helpful during office hours.

She's a great professor. I feel like I'm prepared for the FE, and she gave us extra credit on the exams related to the syllabus! I'd love to have her class again, and if you're debating on taking her, go for it! She curved exams if the average was lower than expected, and recorded lectures so we could revise at any time.

The Best Professor I ever had at UCF! Professor Ali is what all professors should strive to be. She is nothing but caring about her students and so kind. Even though CS1 is a terrifying class for many STEM majors, she made it seem less daunting. I hope to have other classes with her. 100/10.

As a relatively new professor, Dr. Ali blew away my expectations as a Computer Science professor. While she has had a few bumps across the road, she provides a ton of resources, such as C files she writes in lectures and the solutions for assignments. You are graded based on assignments, quizzes, and exams, and the lowest quiz grade is dropped!!
CS1 is usually a hard class however, with this proffesor it was pretty easy and she also prepares you for the FE.

A wonderful professor who care's so much about her students. Clear criteria's for what we need to learn, assignments are well defined, and she offers a lot of material for her students to pass in such a hard class. ABSOLUTELY take her for CS1!
Absolutely amazing professor, she did her best to equip students for success in a difficult class. 100% would take again.

She has her occasional hiccups when it comes to teaching lectures, but she really does care for her students; she provides a lot of studying material for exams, exams usually just go over the rudimentary theory of topics, she'll curve scores if class average is low, and she'll frequently extend due dates. She genuinely wants her students to pass.
My favorite professor so far at UCF. She was extremely kind and made sure to explain thoroughly. She was available to review during office hours. Would definitely reccomend!

One of the best professors I've had at UCF. She is amazing, understanding, caring. She really focuses on her students understanding the material. She is organized, and was always helpful in her office hours. The class is hard but she makes it easy by breaking down hard topic, she really prepares you. Couldn't ask anything more of a professor. 10/10
